entry.title: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content: Hello, I am using the data persistence in Datasphere. Based on the log, the number of records inserted in the table inflates to about 178x so the initial number of records which is 750k becomes 134M. This also inflates the amount of memory being used. Has anyone encountered this? Anyone with data persistence knowledge can share anything more about it? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Using CCM Accrual Engine in S/4HANA 2022 Before Start...

So, I'm really asking if I accrue on my customer's account for payments made to my customer's customer can I "clear" the accruals as rebates are paid?

entry.title: Re: sap business one b1 plant maintenance export
entry.content: In SAP Business One you can export Work Orders and Diagnostic Sheet data to Excel directly. Open the Work Order list in the Plant Maintenance module, then go to Data and click Export to Excel to download the data. For Diagnostic Sheets, open the same screen and use the same export option. If you need more specific data, use Query Generator or Query Manager to fetch the required fields and then export the results to Excel.

entry.title: SAP DM PPD - how to reset/empty string array
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are having some local arrays in our PPDs now once the loop complete, we have to empty the local array for the next iteration. but now the problem is we only have the add array/remove array item in PCo functions.

can anyone please help us if there are items in the array then how we can completely empty array in SAP DM.

Quick help will be appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content: Excellent post, clear and to the point. Is it possible to include APIGEE and WEBMethods in the comparison?

entry.title: Re: Restrict Plant in MB51 Report
entry.content: You can't enhance Selection Screen Events (see the possible positions: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_758_index_htm/7.58/en-US/index.htm?file=abenimplicit_enh_points.htm) but you may implement the Enhancement Point RM07DOCS_01 (Spot ES_RM07DOCS).

entry.title: Entering a long text while creating a dowpayment via FIORI app id F1688
entry.content:
Dear experts, 

I am creating a downpayment via FIORI app id F1688 - Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests. 

And as we have payment details with the text more than 170 symbols the field BSEG-SGTXT doesn't suit our requirement. 

For example, at Create Supplier Down Payment Requests entering a long text is available via Extras ---> Text (please find the screenshot below). 

That would be great if there is any chance to add Extras ---> Text somehow to that fiori app. (F1688)

Maybe we should activate something or take some steps? If it is not possible I would appreciate to get any tips or advices from you.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: MIRO Header Enhancement with custom fields
entry.content: Try BAdI first, because its technology is newer than Customer Exit (CMOD). Also, always refer to SAP official documentation before deciding (SAP Help Portal and SAP Notes).

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC Multiple Cloud Regions
entry.content: If you are using electronic invoices for France, yes according to documentation you need cf-eu02

entry.title: How to Debug the Event Consumption class in SAP BTP ABAP system for an event in Event Mesh ?
entry.content:
Hi All , 

How can i debug the event consumption class in ADT ??

Basically i am trying to consume an event in SAP BTP ABAP system . i have done the necessary objects following this Event Consumption in SAP BTP ABAP   .

1-> Event Consumption Model in SAP BTP ABAP system ( via ADT ) 
2-> Communication Arrangement , Scenario  .

for ex -My IB Communication user is CC00000012 (EVENT_CONSUMER) linked to communication systems for IB services for the Comm arrangement of EventMesh to SAP BTP system based on the custom Communication Scenario . ( Step 9) .

While testing  , i see the event is consumed successfully , however i dont see this Communication user available in F4 help for User External Debugger settings in ADT . I found in ADT ,the F4 help is limited to Dialog user . 

 

Is there any way to debug the event consumption class ?

Thank You .

entry.title: neuer Bewertungsbereich für Versicherungswerte
entry.content:
Guten Tag, können Sie mir bitte helfen zu verstehen, wie der neue Bewertungsbereich für Versicherungswerte angelegt werden muss?

Ich habe jetzt mit allen hier zur Verfügung stehenden Informationen einen Bewertungsbereich erstellt. Dieser bringt den Fehler E006 obwohl ich einen Anlage ohne Wert habe.

Zudem ist mir unklar, wie ich die Versicherungswerte dann von 'migriere' in den neuen Bereich hinein?

Ebenfalls würde ich gerne den Index je Anlagenklasse vorgeben aber das Feld in Transaktion OAYZ ist ausgegraut. 

Gibt es irgendwo eine vernünftige Anleitung?

Vielen Dank im Voraus

S. Horsch

entry.title: Re: Which ledger scenarios are available as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2302?
entry.content:
Hi Verena,

Regarding the "local accounting standard", are you referring to the local accounting standard in other countries? If we are planning to use the parallel ledger and suggest changing the default 0L to the corporate accounting standard?

Also, could you please clarify which corporate accounting standard you are referring to? which specific accounting standard will be applied?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Use of Custom CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE Not Permitted i...
entry.content:
Dear @S_DEV,

No in Public Cloud this should be treated as a standard restriction. YY1_PURCHASE created via Custom Business Object or Custom CDS View belongs to the key user extensibility layer, while RAP in ADT is part of developer extensibility. SAP states that repository objects created with ABAP for Key Users can’t be released for developer extensibility so they cannot be used as direct RAP dependencies

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/533228e1e854433ab16d013f161ca509.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-cloud-background-concepts-and-overview/cloud-optimized-abap-language

https://help.sap.com/doc/112b8531132e43398ed50b2ad38ab14b/latest/en-US/ffa919727c624f25a95bd2f9443244f4.pdf

The supported approach is to either build the RAP model on released developer objects or expose the custom business object through its generated OData service and consume it through a Custom Communication Scenario / API instead of referencing the YY1_* CDS entity directly. SAP documents service generation for Custom Business Objects and the use of the generated YY1_*_CDS service in a custom communication scenario

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/862bb7b0024145db8a55acb7df3684f6/SHIP/en-US/Develop_a_Custom_UI_on_SAP_Cloud_Platform_and_Deploy_it_to_SAP_S4HANA_Cloud.pdf

Summary:

Direct use of YY1_PURCHASE in RAP is not supported in Public Cloud. The supported option is service-based consumption of the key-user object or a RAP model built on released developer objects

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Launch ByDesign UIs from External Applications
entry.content:
@knutheusermann - I realise this is an old thread, but I have found it really useful.

I am trying to determine the URL for both Journal Entry from the Journal Entry ID or UUID and Customer Demand Details from the BOM Line ID or similar? I don't have access to a Developer or Debug Console to be able to find the BO, Namespace etc.

Are you able to share whether these are possible and the URL pattern?

Great post BTW!

entry.title: Re: My Purchase Requisition becomes Advance license
entry.content:
Hello @Kerisha123  & @SenthilkumaranSap 

I have now assigned the app directly again as described.
At the moment, this seems to work, even though we removed the apps that are supposedly dependent (advanced price category). It still works. Are these apps not actually required?

Nevertheless, the change surprised me, as according to the Reference Library only the Private Cloud and On-Premise versions were supposed to be affected by this switch to “Advanced” in Q2.
I also would not have expected this as part of a maintenance update.

Is there a way to track such changes in advance? Or was this not intended in this case?

Best regards
Justin

entry.content:

I have created two ASN.

180000049 – Batch number I have assigned during ASN creation

 

But Batch number creation should not dictate which IBD will be distributed or which IBD should not be disturbed in WM public cloud

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Your User ID: GE307785

Week 1

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hi @Joey3

in this case, I would work with leading and derived roles:

  • Create one leading role which contains all business catalogs and apps that are needed.
  • Then you can create two derived business roles from the leading role.
  • In each derived business role, you can set the restrictions as you need them per role.

See: How to Create Leading and Derived Business Roles | SAP Help Portal

 

Whether you can see the data is controlled by the definition in “Read, Value Help”.

“I try to only set "value help" and maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup. Pls check the screenshot as below. But it's not works, I still can check sales orders that order material isn't maintained the authorization group in product master data.”

→ This is not sufficient, you need to maintain the field Authorization Group as “Read, Value Help”. However, I would suggest to also maintain "Write, Read, Value Help" (see explanation below).

To do so, you need to change the overall Read Access of the role from “Unrestricted” to “Restricted”, then the field definitions for “Read, Value Help” will become visible.

 

 

Answers to each question:

 

1) The authorization group in product master data should be maintained directly with any value or text, is there any other steps for the creation of authorization group?

No, there are no preceding steps.

Once you maintain a value in the field “authorization group” in product master data:

This value automatically becomes available for selection as a restriction value:

 

2) If we only restrict by products level, all other activities are same, can I only maintain "value help" restricted? or "Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help", and "Value Help" all level need to be maintained?

As described above, it is necessary to maintain "Read, Value Help" to define which values can be seen. However, if you set your role’s Write Access to “Unrestricted”, this will override a more specific read permission. Therefore, I would advise you to maintain all three ("Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help", and "Value Help" in each role).

 

3) Can I only maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup, ignore all other setting? like sales area, sales org info.

Yes, this is an either / or situation. You can work with Authorization Group for Product or Authorization Group for Product Group or Authorization Group for Product Type, whichever works best for your specific requirement.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: SAP Learning Management System- Item Completion Assignment Profile Question
entry.content:
For our Leadership Training program, our team has multiple items that all constitute substitute credit for each other.
We want the curricula & item to fall off their profile after they receive credit for any of the other items so in the assignment profile we have the rule below.

Has anyone found a way to select multiple items and add them all at once to this rule? We have 17 items to add to this rule in every single one of the assignment profiles we have built. If there is not a way to select multiple items, has anyone figured out a way to copy and paste the values from one assignment profile rule to the next for this rule? It is not letting me copy and paste either.

 

Appreciate the help!

entry.content: In performance reviews, is it possible to change the icon associated with the rating scale? Currently, stars are displayed, but I'd like to know if there are other options available.

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing in Datasphere?
entry.content: there is no AUDIT_LOG table in the schema DWC_AUDIT_READER. It is DPP_AUDIT_LOG table and it contains audit log for database operations, SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE and DELTE. But doesn't have the information of user activity.

entry.title: Email Template not generating in Q-System after Software Collection Import
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue with a custom Email Template in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have created a custom template in our Development environment and moved it to the Quality system using the Export/Import Software Collections apps. While the template is visible in the "Maintain Email Templates" app in the Q-system, the emails are no longer generating for my business documents.

Could anyone point me toward which specific configuration or mapping is most commonly missed when transporting these objects via Software Collections? Is there a specific activation step required in the Q-system after the import?

Thanks in advance for your help.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: Sizing the SAP Datasphere Object Store
entry.content:
Following the comment from Kristian Appel, can one object store perform with an scenario of Sales orders with 7.5 billion records per month, assuming that that vloumne should include Company code as partition criteria and having 70 company codes for example.  What is the SAP best practise architecture on the new technology with this level of records ?
Kind regards,
Mariano

entry.title: Re: How to change message status from RETRY to FAILED whe...
entry.content:
HI Himani,

Yes — fundamentally,If you use HTTP(S) such that CPI initiates the call, then:

CPI owns:
Execution
Error handling
Retry
FAILED vs COMPLETED status

You can implement Custom retry count or Explicit FAILED statuses

But: Event Mesh does not natively expose queues via HTTP pull the way AMQP does
So practically you cannot “read” an Event Mesh queue via HTTP instead of AMQP

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patch 8 to SP5 patch 4 failed !
entry.content: Andrea, I also had issues helping a customer upgrade to SP5 Patch 4. Some of it was self-inflicted, like not disabling CrowdStrike first. However, we also learned about a clustering issue (SAP KB 3724948), so we opted to wait for SP5 Patch 5, which also resolved CVE identified in the April 2026 Security Note.

Thank you once again for your support and inspiration.

entry.title: Parallel landscape using the separate client
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we are planning to setup and parallel landscape for project line. usually companies use separate system for parallel project line.

can we use new client in same development system for project purpose.  below in the diagram is the approach I am thinking of.

due to budget constrain we can not have more systems. 

what are the risks of cross client object conflicts.  we are on S/4 hana 2023 private cloud edition (RISE).

are there other risks and limitation also?

Your inputs are appreciated.

Regards

entry.title: Re: MIRO Header Enhancement with custom fields
entry.content:
Look first a some OSS notes/KBA such as 1156325 - BAdIs in the Logistics Invoice Verification environment.

  • But I'm not aware of standard MM BAdI (or Exit) to add such customer data to header (only for item with MRM_ITEM_CUSTFIELDS)
  • You could look to FI BaDI such as BADI_FDCB_SUBBAS** (look a not yet implemented BAdI, depending on your exact requirements you may be required to insure some data exchange between MM and FI BadI implementations)

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content: Thank you Jarlei. Appreciate the feedback! I will consider covering them in a follow-up post in future.

entry.title: Re: Maintaining serial numbers during Returns order creation
entry.content:
Hi Thiru,

In the Create Customer Return Without Reference app, you can add serial numbers via Menu -> Extras -> Technical Objects.

The OData V4 API for customer returns was enhanced to support serial numbers with release 2602.

I hope this answers your questions.

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Update on SAP HANA Cloud positioning and commercialization in context of SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Partners, we are hosting a focused live session: Update on SAP HANA Cloud positioning and commercialization in context of SAP Business Data Cloud.

In this session, we walk through how SAP HANA Cloud fits inside SAP Business Data Cloud and what this means for your architecture decisions and client conversations.

We will cover three key areas.  First, how SAP HANA Cloud supports scenarios around agentic AI and custom AI-infused applications.  Second, how the commercial model is integrated within SAP Business Data Cloud.  Third, the main technical differences when accessing SAP HANA Cloud via SAP Business Technology Platform versus SAP Business Data Cloud.

We will also look ahead at upcoming changes in the roadmap so you can align your planning with what is coming next.

If you work in data, analytics, or application development in the SAP ecosystem, this session helps you sharpen how you position SAP HANA Cloud with your customers.

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013682.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPAnalyticsCloud #DataArchitecture #CloudData

entry.title: Re: Issue with POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT, need to select iban
entry.content:
• To select an IBAN for the BP, ask functionals for some rules (currency, country, etc.)
  • Did  you already try to add a record with FNAM value such as 'BBSEG-IBAN' to T_FTPOST table parameter ?
    (should be available since 696488 - RFBIBL00: Batch input data transfer of IBAN data a long time ago)

entry.title: Re: Maintaining serial numbers during Returns order creation
entry.content: Thanks a lot. This helps a lot in proceeding.

entry.title: Re: SF EC/ECP Data transformation with Integration with S...
entry.content:
#GenAI Assisted Content 

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

The following information has been provided by our internal chatbot:

Migration and Data Transformation Considerations

Migrating from an on-prem ECC system to SAP SuccessFactors EC/ECP and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud involves several considerations, especially regarding data transformation and integration.

1. Retro Calculations Across Two Controlling Areas

SAP does not explicitly support retro payroll calculations that span two different controlling areas without transforming historical records. It is crucial to ensure that historical data aligns with the new controlling area to avoid discrepancies during retro calculations.

2. Data Integrity Within EC and ECP

Maintaining historical data with references to the old controlling area post-migration can lead to data integrity issues. SAP recommends aligning all data with the new controlling area structure to ensure a clean configuration for the new system.

3. Payroll Posting File Reconciliation

Without transforming historical data within EC/ECP, there is a risk of inaccuracies in the payroll posting file. Ensuring data transformation before running payroll in ECP and posting results to S4 is essential for accuracy and completeness.

4. Audit Trail and Compliance

Updating historical records to reflect a new controlling area requires careful consideration of audit trails. SAP advises maintaining detailed documentation of changes to ensure compliance and traceability.

5. SAP’s Recommended Approach

SAP recommends transforming historical data within EC/ECP to align with the new S4 controlling area structure at go-live. This transformation is supported and recommended to ensure data consistency and integrity. Utilizing SAP’s Master Data Integration service can facilitate this process. For more information, see Streamlining the Hire-to-Retire Process with SAP One Domain Model .

Tools and Methodologies

SAP provides tools like the SAP Master Data Integration service to assist in data transformation and integration. It is advisable to consult SAP’s documentation and support for detailed guidance on implementing these changes. For more information, see Integrating Employee Central with S/4HANA Cloud: Getting it running and solving issues - Link Collection.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing...
entry.content: Indeed, Security -> Activities should provide you this info.

entry.title: Set employee personnel number ranges in s/4 HANA Public cloud to External
entry.content:
We have a requirement that when a finance vendor payment is being processed, the finance person would like to see the external personnel ID (Workday ID) in the SAP personnel number field on the UI. This would require that the external personnel number range be set to external so that we can populate it with the Workday ID.

How to assign Personnal ID in SAP to external number range

entry.title: Re: Restrict Plant in MB51 Report
entry.content:
Hello- No need to disturb standard MB51 design - you can create a new t.code ZMB51(copying design of MB51) and add your expected design with your 5 plants new logic with error message. Involve ABAP team and GRC team for this requirement on ABAP development along with by having different GRC role.

Regards,

entry.title: AFO Queries with SAP HANA Analytical Privileges
entry.content:
Hi,

Id like to know if the following scenario is possible with SSO. 

I have users logging into AFO to query reports. The reports in question are going to be using SQL Analytical privileges to secure them. The problem I currently have is the technical user SAPHANADB is querying the caluclation view, which means the auth check is being done on the technical user. I would like some sort of principal propagation SSO from AFO, through B4H and into HANA. 

I have checked the DBMS tab in SU01 and both Logon Ticket and Assertion has been ticked. The Kerberos CN is updated in both B4H and HANA. The user also can log into HANA with SSO with Kerberos. 

Is this actually possible with AFO using Analytical Privileges?

entry.title: Issue with DME file
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We use DME structure to generate payment files and initiate bank transfer for Vendors. During this process, we face a peculiar situation wherein, fields "INSTDAMT" and "CCY" are printed in DMEE file without Space.(gets merged)

This causes our Treasury system to reject the whole payment. Out of 50 individual payments, this happens to 2 payments this time. We ran the reprint of DMEE again but got the same result.

Note that this happened earlier week, but on reprint, the issue was corrected. Find enclosed a word document with screen shots:

DMEE TREE: ZFI_TRAX_PAIN001_DE_INT

- DMEE Tree portion relevant for the Node
- DMEE file with error line items highlighted.

Regards
Uday Kumar

entry.title: Re: Detailed Document Search - Not fetching the goal deta...
entry.content:
Hello @Jen_Cooper: We have only EH, E, EH+, EM, and EMM permissions defined on the goal. But previously we don't have F permissions defined on the goal. The support team is asking to incorporate the permission, F, on private and read access to view the goals in Detailed Document Search. But the problem here is, they say that we need to update on all the prior goal templates, if we want to view the performance form of older templates. This is what makes the latest experience more tedious as we have several years of templates and we need to update all those as we don't know what auditors ask.

We sent back the ticket for an alternate solution on this and still waiting for the same.

entry.title: Stock replication trigger behavior in SAP_COM_0447
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are evaluating the SAP_COM_0447 – Material Stock Quantity Replication scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Our requirement is to send stock updates to an external system whenever there is a stock change (e.g. goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting).

However, based on our understanding and initial tests:

  • The service seems to work as an asynchronous replication (snapshot-based) rather than event-driven
  • After a goods movement (e.g. GR), the outbound service is not triggered immediately

    We would like to clarify:

    1. Does SAP_COM_0447 automatically trigger for every stock movement?
    2. Is there any standard way to trigger replication on stock change (event-based)?
    3. What is the recommended approach if near real-time stock updates are required?

      Any insights or project experiences would be appreciated.

      Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Observer Design Pattern – Real-Time Use Cases
entry.content: Moderator notified, asking to move this from Q&A to Blog Posts.

entry.title: Re: Observer Design Pattern – Real-Time Use Cases
entry.content: Another solution is to use Events -> Decoupled and handled by kernel, i.e. these ABAP keywords: EVENTS, RAISE EVENT, METHODS ... FOR EVENT ..., SET HANDLER.

entry.title: Re: How to access SAP BTP cockpit without a global or sub...
entry.content:
Hello,

Has long has you gave access to to SAPforMe has Admin and and the Global Account is listed under Systems & Provistions - Systems, you can add a NEW Global Account Admin from here

SEE instructions :

https://me.sap.com/notes/2669325

I can confirmed it worked since I just done it now : If there is no user with BTP Global Account Administrator role

And then the New Global Account simply appears when using the Switch Global Account in BTP Cockpit.

 

Hope it helps!

 

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: User: AC300864U251

entry.title: Re: Detailed Document Search - Not fetching the goal deta...
entry.content: Why don't you use this permission then if you have EH in your templates? "Extended EH for Performance Goals is accessible through Manage Permission Roles Administrator Permissions Manage Goals and can be assigned separately for each goal plan template" if the person pulling the detailed document search has it they should be able to see goals

entry.title: Re: Adding Legacy Main Asset Number to Asset History Shee...
entry.content:
Dear @Daniel_Fedi,

In Public Cloud adding Legacy Main Asset Number directly to the standard Asset History Sheet app is currently not supported in standard. SAP explicitly states in KBA 3481921 that the Asset History Sheet app currently provides no field or filter for legacy assets. SAP documented workaround is to identify the relevant assets in Manage Legacy Assets and then analyze them in Asset History Sheet by filtering on Fixed Asset

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3481921

From a data perspective, SAP documents that the migrated legacy asset number is stored in the Original Asset field on the Origin tab of the asset master. In parallel the Public Cloud extensibility model supports data source extensions for enabling the usage of existing fields in predelivered data sources and it supports custom CDS views and custom analytical queries for additional reporting requirements. Therefore if the requirement is to display the legacy number explicitly as a reporting field the supported Public Cloud approach is to build a separate custom analytical report based on released sources rather than trying to inject the field into the standard Asset History Sheet app itself

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3ccb50e724b045508fea8b2cf1774b2b.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2602.500/en-US/FSD_CE2602.pdf

Summary:

The standard Asset History Sheet app does not currently support Legacy Main Asset Number as a field or filter. The supported options are the SAP workaround via Manage Legacy Assets + Fixed Asset filter or a custom analytical report if the legacy number must be shown explicitly

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
We’re announcing something new to celebrate the people who keep conversations going in the SAP Community. And we need your vote on a badge refresh.

Every month, thousands of discussions happen here. And they happen because someone, (maybe you?) took a few minutes to help out. Maybe it’s a quick tip you’ve learned the hard way, a workaround that saved your project, or just jumping into a tricky technical discussion to hash things out together.

Your contributions matter. They are what make this community what it is - a vibrant, collaborative space where everyone can learn and grow.

We wanted to find a new way to recognize your efforts so I’m thrilled to announce the roll out of Reply Progression Badges! To celebrate your contributions to the SAP Community, one reply at a time. The badges will launch Wednesday, April 22!

What Are Reply Progression Badges?
We’ve created a brand new badge set that celebrates your journey as an active community contributor. These badges are earned automatically when you reach specific thresholds of published replies on forum posts and discussions throughout the SAP Community.

Here are the milestones (replies given):

Replies given

How It Works - Earning the badges is simple:

  1. Find content that interests you – Browse developer forums and discussions across any topic area you’re interested in.
  2. Share your thoughts – Reply with your insights, experiences, questions, or feedback. Your perspective matters!
  3. Watch your progress – As your reply count grows, you’ll automatically unlock badges at each milestone.

Important note: Only replies on forum posts and discussions count for these badges. Replies on other content types, like events or questions, are not included in this set.

Something I’m really excited to let you know, is that you don’t have to start from zero! These badges are awarded retroactively, which means all your past forum and discussion replies already count. If you’ve been an active community member and have already published 200 replies, you’ll automatically receive all the badges up to that milestone. Again, we'll launch the set on Wednesday, April 22. The launch will take place over several hours - since they are retroactive we don't want to overrun the system. Please be patient:)

To check your current reply count, go to your My Content page, and scroll down to the Private Statistics. You’ll find your Discussion and Forum replies count there.

With only 39 replies to my name so far, I’ll be working toward the full progression myself! It’s a good reminder that we’re all on this journey together.

Once earned, your new badges will be on your My Content > Badges page. If you want to show them off, you can add your favorite badges to your content signature, so they are displayed next to your name on your posts and replies across the community. Check your My Content > Badges page today to see which badges you’ve already earned!

 

Replies Matter!
I’m really excited to launch this badge progression because every authentic contribution enriches our community. When you reply to a post, you’re doing more than just responding, you’re

  • Helping others solve problems
  • Building connections
  • Contributing to our collective expertise
  • Learning yourself 

Your experience might be exactly what someone needs. Great discussions and connections start with engaged replies. Future members will benefit from the insights you share today, and discussing concepts will help deepen your own understanding.

Over my years working on the community reputation program, I’ve seen first-hand how a single thoughtful reply can make someone’s day, or even their project outcome. The best replies are authentic. React to what resonates with you. Share what you genuinely know. Ask clarifying questions when you’re curious. Agree, respectfully disagree, or build on ideas.

One More Thing, Can You Help Us Add Some Color!?
Regarding our previous progression badges, we’ve heard your feedback loud and clear, they could use a refresh. Many of you mentioned that the badges all look too similar because they use the same background color. I have to agree now that I see them all on one badge profile.

 

So it’s time to make each badge set more distinctive and exciting! We’re planning to update the color on the following five progression badge sets that were launched last year:

  1. Blogs Published
  2. Discussions Published
  3. Likes Given
  4. Likes Received
  5. Answers Accepted as Solutions

Here’s where we need YOUR help!

We want each badge set to have its own unique color from the SAP brand color palette. The available color family options are:

Gray

Indigo

Teal

Green

Raspberry

Pink

Mango

Red

Since we have five badge sets to update, we’ll select the five most popular colors based on your votes.

How to vote: Simply reply to this blog post with your color preferences! List your top choices or just name your favorites. I’ll tally the votes from the comments and announce the winning colors in a follow-up post. I can’t wait to see which colors rise to the top, and I’ll be casting my own vote in the comments too!

In conclusion, the SAP Community thrives because of members like you who engage, share, and support each other. With our new Reply Progression Badges, we’re celebrating the power of conversation and recognizing those who make our community better, one reply at a time.

So act now, check your Private Statistics to see where you stand, claim your retroactive badges, and keep the conversation going! And don’t forget to give your color votes in the comments below. I can’t wait to see which colors win!

Have questions about the new badges or the color vote? Let me know in the comments. I’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Re: Supplier not getting assigned to purchase requisition...
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the detailed inputs. We have the complete setup available, problem is even after contract was created last year itself, for some materials system did not assign supplier to pur req.

entry.title: Re: 1H Release 2026 Enhancements in Business Configuratio...
entry.content:
Hi,

Business Configuration UI is used to maintain Succession Data Model and CSF Succession Data Model. So, it should not affect MDF objects configured through Configure Object Definition.

Now, if you are talking about Configurable UIs created for MDF objects, then you may have impacts when enabling the latest Viewing, Editing and History UI, especially if you created UI rules. The recommendation in this case is to wait for the next release to use the  latest Viewing, Editing and History UI.

entry.title: Serial Number obligatory material cannot be updated error in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Why do we receive the Error "Serial number obligatory material cannot be updated" When using Confirm Production Order app in S/4HANA Public Cloud


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Serial Number obligatory material cannot be updated e...
entry.content:
Please refer to the documentation 'Confirm Production Order' and Confirm Production Order (CO15)

 

Serial number handling is not supported for confirmations.

 

Use Material Management apps to post goods movements. For example use app Post Goods Movement  (MIGO) to post goods receipt or goods issue for the production order with serial numbers.

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing...
entry.content: Thank you, is there any table/view that stores the information of Security -> Activities. So that I can prepare a model on that get a report. Instead of downloading it manually every time from this activities page?

entry.title: Re: Stock replication trigger behavior in SAP_COM_0447
entry.content:
Hi Melis_Sorgun,

That comm arrangement is more for replication as you have stated, it also does not support deltas

Help:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/7d77050a7f084207affb68455b5b7b13.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

 

Have you considered using Event (and Event Mesh) - we have material documents events:

https://api.sap.com/event/CE_MATERIALDOCUMENTEVENTS/overview

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/40783449115e4b53a9da6e513caf8f50.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

 

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Exchange rate differences posting issue in bank reconciliation in FF_5
entry.content:
Dear All,

Please be informed that we have encountered a scenario where an invoice was posted in foreign currency (KWD) for 1,000.000, with an equivalent value of USD 3,300.00. Upon receiving the bank statement, we noticed that the bank charged USD 3,310.00. While performing the online bank reconciliation (BRS) (MT940), the system generated the error message: ‘Error F5 263 – The difference is too large for clearing.’

Kindly refer to the attached documents and provide your guidance on this issue. Your support in resolving this matter would be highly appreciated.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Demystified: Legacy System Migration Workbench vs. SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi @Ronald_Amigo
We are now at FPS1 in 2025 and the LSMW is still be usable. They are no plans as far as I know to retire the transaction. As write above the LSMW is only a tool to run any kind of migration methods. I guess you are using batch input/transaction recordings for your changes in 2022. After the upgrade you must always verify if your recordings are still working as expected. There is always a likelihood that processes got renewed by application development and that transactions got retired or that they may change their functionality or screens that then invalidates former recordings. You may also check if mass maintenance apps are existing for a specific use case. That is normally the best for Mass maintenance on master data changes. You may also try above described iRPA or try an AI bot.

Br

Frank

entry.title: No Bell icon notification for Project Billing Request Workflow
entry.content:
Hello,

I, as a Project Manager is not receiving any notification (the bell icon) for Project Billing Request Approval, although I get a task in my inbox when I go the SAP Homepage.

Manage Workflow for Project Billing Request

By default, the approvers are informed by means of notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services

entry.title: Re: People Intelligence Reports
entry.content:
I could not find anything in the roadmap.

You can track future release contents from this link: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=089E017A62AB1EDA94C15F5EDB3320E1&PRODUCT=73554900100800006262&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q2%202026 

As People Intelligence is part of SAP Business Data Cloud, it is possible to create a custom solution, but probably will need to engage multidisciplinay experts (SAC, Datasphere, SAP Databricks).

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA federated roles assignment to users in BTP via SAP Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
Excellent blog.

I believe, step 5 is not necessary if you are provisioning the user/roles in IPS. You don't need to perform any manual mapping of IAS groups to BTP roles because when you choose the toggle for IPS to provision the users in the channel manager settings, the roles will never come to BTP after content exposure. 

 

The little information icon gives this info.

entry.title: Re: SAP By Design Users in Scan to Build
entry.content: Scan to Build is not a standard feature of ByD. Are you referring to any specific Add-on? For Example: Solution Name: scan4cloud?

entry.title: Multiple External Vendors - PP02 - SIngle Material Number
entry.content:
I am hoping if someone can tell me 1) if this is possible and 2) if you either describe how to or show me where to look, i would be greatful.,

 

I have one material number, it has both internal and multiple external processes, in the routings, the external process have been set to PP02.

 

1) Is it possible to track where the stock is at each external vendor (and how do I do this) and 

2) Is this possible to do with multiple external vendors on one material number.

3) What are my options when it comes to getting PO created for the second external process when the item doesnt come back to the company after the the first process.

entry.title: Re: ManageContactIN Api
entry.content:
Dear Mark,
I hope you’re doing well.

For examples of the payload used by the ManageContactIn web service, we recommend reviewing the following official SAP documentation:

Inbound Service Manage Contacts
Create contact persons using Web service APIs
Help Portal - Web Services

If, after reviewing the documentation, you continue to encounter errors, please feel free to reopen the SAP Support ticket where you received the AI-generated response. Once reopened, the case will be forwarded to our support team so we can review the payload in detail and, if needed, test the scenario further to determine the root cause of the issue.

Thank you for your understanding.

Kind regards,
Saulo.
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Joule Studio Skill Fails with 403 (Access Denied) During Execution
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on a Joule Studio skill in SAP Build that consumes data from SAP Datasphere via an OData V4 service.

The solution was working correctly at an earlier stage, but recently we started encountering an issue during execution.

When running the skill, the request fails with the following behavior:

  • HTTP status: 403 (Access Denied)
  • The execution does not complete successfully and no data is returned

What we have verified so far:

  1. The OData service works correctly when tested independently
  2. The destination is properly configured and reachable
  3. The skill logic and parameter mapping are correct
  4. Joule correctly identifies the scenario and extracts parameters
    No significant changes were made between the working state and the current issue

Observed behavior:

  • The backend service is not reached
  • Output variables return null
  • Additional errors appear during result processing due to missing response data

Environment:

  • SAP Build (Joule Studio)
  • SAP Datasphere (OData V4)
  • Integration through Joule

At this point, the issue seems to occur specifically during the execution phase, but we have not been able to identify the root cause.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or has suggestions on what could be causing this behavior?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated, as this issue is currently blocking our progress.

Thank you very much in advance for your help.

entry.title: Using REST polling for pulling file from Azure blob storage PI/PO 7.5 SP 34
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to pull a file from Azure blob storage using REST sender with REST Polling. I have tried with SAS URL, but getting error "HTTP GET call to htpps://XXX not successful. Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature"

The authorization values in SAS URL is as follows "sv=2025-11-05&ss=bfqt&srt=o&sp=rwdlacupiytfx&se=2027-04-21"

I am using x-ms-version 2022-11-02 in HTTP Header

The call with the same URL from Postman is successful.

Is the REST Sender adapter capable of pulling file from Azure storage?

Is there any additional configuration other than the above required for making this work.

Thank you

entry.title: There is a bulk upload in Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) in ATP for product substitution
entry.content: There is a Fiori app for bulk uploading product replacements

entry.title: A2A Agents on SAP BTP: Wiring the Agent-to-Agent Protocol into CAP
entry.content:
A2A Agents on SAP BTP: Wiring the Agent-to-Agent Protocol into CAP

A technical guide to building Agent-to-Agent agents natively on SAP Business Technology Platform

Google's Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol is quickly becoming a standard for agent interoperability. For SAP developers, this raises a practical question: how do you build A2A agents that feel native to BTP — authenticated through XSUAA, persisting state to HANA, reaching services through destinations — without reinventing infrastructure that already exists?

The answer might be simpler than expected: mount A2A directly into a CAP application. Not as a sidecar. As a first-class part of your server, sharing its runtime, its database, its service bindings. This post explores what that architecture looks like using TypeScript and the @a2a-js/sdk.

This will hopefully become a short blog series, from this introduction to the fully working project template.

The Project Structure

A CAP-based A2A agent follows a familiar CAP layout, with the agent logic living alongside standard services:

cap-based-agent/
├── db/
│   └── schema.cds                  # HANA entities for tasks, telemetry
├── srv/
│   ├── a2a-service.cds             # Read-only CDS service for monitoring
│   ├── a2a-service.ts              # Service handler (if needed)
│   ├── server.ts                   # CAP bootstrap — mounts A2A routes
│   ├── lib/
│   │   └── base-executor.ts        # Reusable A2A task lifecycle engine
│   └── agent/
│       ├── index.ts                # Your agent subclass
│       ├── agent-definition.ts     # Declarative agent config
│       └── agent-card.ts           # A2A agent card (discovery metadata)

If you've built a CAP application before, this looks familiar — and that's the point. The agent is a CAP app. cds watch works. cds deploy -2 hana creates your HANA tables. cds up ships everything as one artifact.

One Server, Two Protocol Worlds

CAP generates an Express application at startup. The cds.on("bootstrap") hook gives you access to that Express instance before CAP finalizes its own routes — and that's where A2A plugs in:

// server.ts
cds.on("bootstrap", async (app: express.Application) => {
    const agentExecutor = new agentDefinition.executorClass(agentDefinition.config);
    const hanaTaskStore = new HanaTaskStore();
    const requestHandler = new DefaultRequestHandler(
        agentDefinition.agentCard, hanaTaskStore, agentExecutor
    );

    // A2A discovery endpoint
    app.use(`${agentDefinition.path}/.well-known/agent.json`,
        agentCardHandler({ agentCardProvider: requestHandler }));

    // A2A JSON-RPC transport (the one Joule speaks)
    app.use(`${agentDefinition.path}/a2a/jsonrpc`,
        express.json(), jwtMiddleware,
        jsonRpcHandler({ requestHandler }));

    console.log(`🚀 Agent "${agentDefinition.name}" is running`);
});

After this, your application speaks A2A's JSON-RPC on one path and CAP's OData/REST on the others. One deployment, one HANA binding, one XSUAA instance. The operational surface area doesn't grow.

Persistence: Just CDS

An A2A agent needs to store tasks, push notification configurations, and — if you care about observability — telemetry data. In CAP, that's a schema file:

-- db/schema.cds
entity Tasks : managed {
    key id        : String(36);
        contextId : String(36);
        state     : String(20);
        data      : LargeString;
}

entity AgentTaskTelemetry : managed {
    key taskId           : UUID;
        contextId        : String(100);
        finalState       : String(30);
        durationMs       : Integer;
        toolsCalled      : Integer default 0;
        tokensTotal      : Integer default 0;
        errorMessage     : String(500);
}

The managed aspect gives you createdAt, modifiedAt, createdBy for free. Expose the telemetry entity as a read-only CAP service and you get a monitoring API without writing a single handler:

-- srv/a2a-service.cds
service A2AServerService @(path: '/a2a-service') {
    @readonly entity Tasks as projection on db.Tasks excluding { data };
    @readonly entity AgentTaskTelemetry as projection on db.AgentTaskTelemetry;
}

Query your agent's performance, error rates, and token consumption through standard OData. No custom dashboards required to get started.

Extending A2A Standards with CAP: The HanaTaskStore

The A2A SDK defines a TaskStore interface — a contract for persisting task state. The default in-memory implementation works for prototyping, but production agents on BTP need HANA. Here's where CAP shines: implementing a HanaTaskStore becomes almost trivial because you're working with CDS, not around it.

The TaskStore interface expects methods like get, set, and delete. With CAP's query language, each of these maps to a one-liner:

async get(taskId: string): Promise {
    const result = await SELECT.one.from("a2a.taskstore.Tasks").where({ id: taskId });
    return result ? JSON.parse(result.data) : undefined;
}

async set(task: Task): Promise {
    await UPSERT.into("a2a.taskstore.Tasks").entries({
        id: task.id,
        contextId: task.contextId,
        state: task.status.state,
        data: JSON.stringify(task),
    });
}

No connection pools to configure. No ORM mappings to maintain. CDS handles the HANA connection lifecycle through the same binding your CAP services already use. The same pattern extends naturally: a HanaPushNotificationStore for managing notification configs, a HanaCheckpointer for LangGraph's conversation memory — each one a thin adapter between an SDK interface and a CDS entity. This is the kind of extensibility that emerges when your agent framework and your persistence layer share the same runtime.

The Base Executor: Lifecycle Without Ceremony

The heart of the architecture is a base executor class that handles the full A2A task lifecycle. It manages LLM streaming, publishes status updates through the A2A event bus, handles interrupts when the agent needs user input, and deals with cancellation and timeouts. Here's what it looks like, simplified to the essential flow:

// lib/base-executor.ts (simplified)
export abstract class BaseAgentExecutor implements AgentExecutor {

    async execute(requestContext: RequestContext, eventBus: ExecutionEventBus): Promise<void> {
        const { taskId, contextId, userMessage } = requestContext;

        // 1. Publish "working" status
        this.publishStatusUpdate(eventBus, taskId, contextId, "working", "Processing...", false);

        // 2. Build messages — system prompt on first turn only, LangGraph
        //    prepends full checkpoint history on follow-ups automatically
        const messages = await this.buildMessages(userMessage, contextId);

        // 3. Stream the agent's response
        const stream = await this.agent.stream(
            { messages },
            { configurable: { thread_id: contextId } }
        );

        let lastAIContent = "";
        for await (const chunk of stream) {
            // Agent is asking the user a question — pause and wait
            if (chunk.__interrupt__?.length) {
                const question = this.getInputRequiredMessage(taskId, chunk.__interrupt__[0].value);
                this.publishStatusUpdate(eventBus, taskId, contextId, "input-required", question, false);
                return; // Checkpoint preserved — conversation resumes on next message
            }

            // Capture latest AI output
            if (isAIMessage(chunk)) {
                lastAIContent = chunk.content;
                this.publishStatusUpdate(eventBus, taskId, contextId, "working",
                    this.getWorkingMessage(taskId, lastAIContent), false);
            }
        }

        // 4. Publish final result and persist telemetry
        this.publishArtifact(eventBus, taskId, contextId, lastAIContent);
        this.publishStatusUpdate(eventBus, taskId, contextId, "completed",
            this.getCompletedMessage(taskId, lastAIContent), true);
    }
}

The key states are clear: working → input-required → working → completed (or failed/canceled). The base class manages HANA-backed conversation checkpointing for multi-turn dialogues, abort controllers with configurable timeouts, concurrency guards preventing parallel writes to the same thread, and automatic telemetry persistence after every execution. All of this stays invisible to the subclass author.

Your Agent: Override What Matters

Building a concrete agent means extending the base and providing just the behavior-specific pieces:

// agent/index.ts
export default class GreetingsAgentExecutor extends BaseAgentExecutor {
    constructor(config?: Partial<AgentConfig>) {
        super({ loggerName: "GreetingsAgentExecutor", ...config });
    }

    protected getWorkingMessage(taskId: string, thoughts: string): string {
        return `Working on it. ${thoughts || ""}`;
    }

    protected getCompletedMessage(taskId: string, content: string): string {
        return content;
    }

    protected getInputRequiredMessage(_taskId: string, interruptValue: unknown): string {
        if ((interruptValue as any)?.type === "ask_user") {
            return (interruptValue as any).question;
        }
        return "I need more information to proceed.";
    }
}

And then, the definition ties everything together declaratively:

// agent/agent-definition.ts
export const agentDefinition: AgentDefinition = {
    name: "greetings-agent",
    path: "/greetings-agent",
    agentCard: exampleAgentCard,
    executorClass: GreetingsAgentExecutor,
    config: {
        systemPrompt: "You are a friendly assistant that greets users using ASCII Code Art",
        orchestration: new OrchestrationClient({
            promptTemplating: { model: { name: "gpt-4o" } },
        }, { streaming: true }, { resourceGroup: "default" }, {
            destinationName: "ai-core-wr-artificial-intelligence-sdk",
        }),
    },
};

No API keys in environment variables. The BTP Destination Service handles credentials for AI Core access. The same mechanism works for any LLM available through the Orchestration API.

Rather than managing separate LLM integrations and credentials, we use the Orchestration Client abstracts away model selection, prompt templating, and fallback policies. Your agent configuration simply declares which model to use, and the Orchestration API handles routing, token counting, cost tracking, and seamless model switching without code changes. This makes agents portable across SAP's AI landscape — from open-source models to enterprise-licensed capabilities — while keeping sensitive model endpoints and API keys secure within the BTP infrastructure.

Leveraging the sap-ai-sdk/orchestration SDK, Agents can even leverage pre-existing Orchestration configuration: Prompt Engineers prepare the scenario in SAP AI Launchpad, developers wire it up into Agents with a simple ID.

MCP Tool Discovery Through BTP Destinations

An agent that only talks to an LLM is limited. The real power comes when it can call external tools. The architecture supports connecting to MCP servers through BTP destinations — add them to your agent definition and tools are discovered at startup:

config: {
    mcpServers: [{
        name: "My Tool Server",
        destinationName: "destination-to-mcp-server",
        path: "/mcp",
    }]
}

Each tool call could use principal propagation enabled destinations, so authorization is enforced per request:

// We can customize how the StreamableHTTPClientTransport triggers its fetches by providing a customized fetch function:
const baseFetch = (transportOpts.fetch as typeof globalThis.fetch) ?? globalThis.fetch;
transportOpts.fetch = async (input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit) => {
	const mergedHeaders = new Headers(init?.headers);
	try {
		const userJwt = getCurrentJwt();
		const dest = await getDestination({
			destinationName: serverConfig.destinationName,
			...(userJwt && { jwt: userJwt }),
		});

		if (dest?.authTokens?.length) {
			for (const token of dest.authTokens) {
				if (!token.error && token.http_header) {
					mergedHeaders.set(token.http_header.key, token.http_header.value);
				}
			}
		}
		// Additional headers needed by the destination?
		if (dest?.headers) {
			for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(dest.headers)) {
				mergedHeaders.set(key, value as string);
			}
		}
	} catch (err) {
		this.LOG.warn(
			`Failed to resolve destination "${serverConfig.destinationName}" for tool call:`,
			err instanceof Error ? err.message : err,
		);
	}

The inbound JWT can be safely stored in a node's AsyncLocalStorage: the a2a-sdk/js uses Express, so a simple middleware will suffice:

export function createJwtContextMiddleware(xsuaaService: XsuaaService | null) {
   return function jwtContextMiddleware(req: Request, _res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
   const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
   if (!authHeader?.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
	next();
	return;
   }
   const token = authHeader.substring(7);
   // Do something with the JWT
}

Wired up to the a2a jsonrpc endpoint:

// JSON-RPC endpoint with middleware chain
app.use(
  `${agentPath}/a2a/jsonrpc`,
  express.json(),
  jwtMiddleware, // Extract the JWT before the a2a's jsonRpc handler
  jsonRpcHandler({ requestHandler, userBuilder }),
);

A Note on Language Choice

You can build A2A agents in any language — the protocol is transport-level JSON-RPC, so Python, Go, Java, anything that speaks HTTP will work. Python in particular has a rich ecosystem for AI workloads and may be the natural choice for teams with existing ML pipelines.

But it's worth considering what comes after the LLM call. An agent on BTP needs to persist tasks somewhere. It needs to authenticate users. It needs to resolve destinations for external services, manage push notification tokens, store conversation checkpoints. In a Python-based setup, each of these is a separate integration to build and maintain: an SAP HANA client library, manual XSUAA token validation, a custom Destination Service client, connection pooling, credential lifecycle management.

With CAP, these are solved problems. cds.run(SELECT.from(...)) talks to HANA through an already-configured binding. JWT verification hooks into the CAP middleware stack. Destinations resolve through the SAP Cloud SDK. The managed aspect on your CDS entities handles audit fields you'd otherwise write by hand.

The question isn't whether Python or other languages can do all of this — they can. The question is whether you want to spend your time wiring infrastructure or building agent behavior. When the platform already provides a programming model that handles the plumbing, there's real value in staying within it.

See the SAP Architecture Center article for additional reference.

What's Next

This architecture opens up interesting possibilities. Imagine agents that can leverage CDS-defined actions as tools, or that use CAP's ecosystem integrations to react to business events autonomously. The base executor pattern means each new agent is a subclass and a config object — the protocol and infrastructure complexity stays solved once.

Next, we'll dive deeper in the initial setup of the major building blocks to wire up the a2a standards with HANA.

Co-Authored with Haiku 🙂

HI

Please provide the step with example How to Setup supplier invoice email trigger process in work flow

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: Re: Will try to submit to MS. Are you suggesting that Microsoft posted the sample code that you provided? Where did you find it? I am surprised that MS would provide an example that has an odd-length buffer size.

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Handeling Multi-Turn Conversations
entry.content: Awesome post felixbartler. Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: SAP PP/DS for Process Industry - Setup Time
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Setup matrix works for me in discrete manufacturing, but not in process industry (PP‑PI).

Using recipe + CRC resource in PP/DS:

  • setup matrix assigned to resource
  • recipe creates only one activity
  • no setup activity, no green setup bar

     

  • SAPC00 used in standard value key and also setup time (SAP_01), but PP/DS still creates only one activity.

Question:
How is a setup activity created in PP‑PI so that the setup matrix can be applied?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform API Management layer.
entry.content:
After looking into this further and weighing the pros and cons, I’ve decided on a path that balances governance with performance. I wanted to share it here in case it helps others.

The Conclusion: APIM by Default We’ve decided to stick with SAP BTP API Management as our primary gateway for both internal and external traffic. While bypassing it for internal apps seems tempting for speed, the 'extra hop' latency is actually negligible (usually just a few milliseconds). The risk of losing central visibility and security control is a much bigger concern than the minor speed gain.

A Simple Example of Why:

  • The Problem: If we let internal apps call S/4HANA directly, we lose the ability to see who is calling what. If an internal app suddenly starts sending too many requests, it could slow down the entire S/4 system for everyone.

  • The Solution: By keeping them in APIM, we can set a 'Quota' policy. This acts like a safety valve—if an app goes over its limit, APIM catches it before it ever hits our core S/4 system.

How we are handling the 'Internet' concern: For our most sensitive internal traffic, we are looking into SAP BTP Private Link. This gives us the best of both worlds: we get the full governance and analytics of API Management, but the data travels over a private connection rather than the public internet.

Final Rule of Thumb: We will use APIM for everything to keep a 'Clean Core.' We will only allow a direct connection as a rare exception for massive data transfers where every millisecond is critical, but those will be documented case-by-case.

entry.title: Display corresponding infotype settings when switching between different countries in PA30.
entry.content:
1、We have a global project with users in three regions: Chinese mainland, Taiwan region of China, and Vietnam.

2、Display country grouping–specific infotype settings when switching among country groupings 99, 42, and 28 in PA30.

3、How to enable a user to switch between country/region-specific infotype displays in PA30 after logging on.

For example, when viewing an employee in Vietnam and selecting the relevant country grouping, the infotype display can be switched to show the infotypes under country grouping 99.

4、I have attempted to configure it using the settings below, but it did not work.

entry.title: Re: Compensation template calc fields
entry.content:
Hi, for the system-calculated fields, the Implementation Guide on the SAP Help portal is a very valuable resource. This is the link to the one for Compensation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-compensation/implementing-and-managing-compensation/standard-columns-in-compensation-plan-template

You can do a CTRL+F or use the search at the top to find the information about the columns you're looking for. There is a similar guide for Variable Pay as well.

entry.title: Re: Stock replication trigger behavior in SAP_COM_0447
entry.content:
Dear @Melis_Sorgun,

Question 1
No. SAP_COM_0447 / Material Stock Replicate is documented as a SOAP outbound replication service. SAP Public Cloud inventory API catalog lists Material Stock Replicate, Material Stock Read and MaterialDocumentCreateNotification_Async_Out as separate artifacts so SAP_COM_0447 should not be positioned as the standard immediate trigger for every GR, GI or transfer posting

Question 2
Use the material document notification pattern rather than the stock replication service itself. In the same Public Cloud inventory API area SAP documents MaterialDocumentCreateNotification_Async_Out separately from stock replication which supports treating the material document as the triggering object when inventory postings occur

Question 3
The recommended Public Cloud design is use material document notification as the trigger, identify the affected material / plant / storage location then call Material Stock Read to retrieve the updated stock quantity and forward that result to the external system. If the landscape requires event distribution or buffering Public Cloud also supports integration with SAP Event Mesh

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/1891d652a8d54a46a587bc11a0a713c7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6ef7f849fed34f95adfc449f29835255/f5cb5d8e09db4e609d1882a25ec0ee39.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/7d77050a7f084207affb68455b5b7b13.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implement-an-integration-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-with-sap-commerce-cloud/setting-up-the-integration-configuration_cc037ffe-9b4a-47a5-ba7f-6921d4b8c3bb

Best Regards,

Dawid
